title: Nobody Expected a Fish to Walk on Land for 4 Days
script: This fish survives completely out of water — and it hunts on land. The snakehead fish, native to Southeast Asia, can breathe air directly and drag itself across land using its fins. It travels up to 400 meters between ponds, surviving 4 full days on dry ground. But here's the twist — it doesn't just survive. It actively chases prey on land, like a fish that forgot it's a fish. Send this to someone who still thinks fish can't leave the water. And that's why.
search_summary: Snakehead fish can breathe air, walk on land for 4 days, and hunt prey outside water.

title: Crows Remember Your Face — And Hold Grudges
script: One bird recognizes your face, tells its children about you, and passes that grudge down for generations. Crows identify individual human faces — proven in a 2008 University of Washington study where masked researchers who trapped crows got dive-bombed for years afterward. The crows that attacked weren't even the ones originally caught. Their offspring joined in. But here's what's unsettling: crows also remember kindness the same way. They've been documented leaving gifts — bottle caps, shiny objects — for specific humans who fed them. So technically, you already have a reputation with every crow in your neighborhood. And this is how.
search_summary: Crows recognize and remember individual human faces, hold long-term grudges, and teach their offspring to target specific people.
comment_question: Have you ever felt like a crow was specifically watching YOU? 🐦
